summary: Verified and FIXED two critical bugs in V1 In-App Messaging: 1) Messages now appear in athlete's inbox (root cause: UserOut Pydantic model missing athlete_id field), 2) Message-sent events now display in Support Pod timeline (was already working after previous fix). All 8 backend tests pass. All frontend flows verified working.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed UserOut model in /app/backend/models.py to include athlete_id: Optional[str] = None
Fixed MeResponse model in /app/backend/models.py to include athlete_id: Optional[str] = None
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_BugFix_ prefixed message threads during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: In-App Messaging V1 fully working. Root cause of bug #1: Pydantic UserOut model did not include athlete_id field, so even though _safe_user() added it, Pydantic stripped it from response. Fix: Added athlete_id: Optional[str] = None to UserOut and MeResponse models. Bug #2 (timeline events) was already working - ActivityHistory.js has TYPE_META for support_message_sent and support_message_reply.

bug_1_athlete_inbox.issue: Messages sent by coach not appearing in athlete inbox
bug_1_athlete_inbox.root_cause: UserOut Pydantic model in /app/backend/models.py did not include athlete_id field. The _safe_user() function correctly added athlete_id to the dict, but Pydantic filtered it out during serialization
bug_1_athlete_inbox.fix: Added 'athlete_id: Optional[str] = None' to UserOut and MeResponse models
bug_1_athlete_inbox.verification: GET /api/auth/login now returns athlete_id in user object, GET /api/support-messages/inbox returns threads for athlete

bug_2_timeline_events.issue: Message-sent events not appearing in Support Pod timeline
bug_2_timeline_events.status: Already working - ActivityHistory.js has support_message_sent and support_message_reply in TYPE_META
bug_2_timeline_events.verification: Timeline shows 'Coach Williams sent message: ...' entries when filtered to Messages

backend_api: test_01_coach_login - Coach login returns valid token
test_02_athlete_login - Athlete login returns token WITH athlete_id=athlete_3
test_03_coach_sends_message - POST /api/support-messages returns message_id, thread_id, status=sent
test_05_athlete_inbox_has_thread - GET /api/support-messages/inbox contains thread just created
test_support_message_types_in_timeline - Timeline action_events include support_message types
frontend_ui: Emma Chen athlete card visible in Athletes Requiring Attention
Support Pod page loads with action bar (Log, Email, Follow-up, Notes, Escalate)
Athlete login as Emma Chen successful
Messages sidebar nav item shows unread badge (17)
Thread list shows unread indicators (teal dots and count badges)
Thread detail view opens with conversation
Reply input and send button present
Timeline shows support_message_sent and support_message_reply events
